-- See Hoogle, http://www.haskell.org/hoogle/


-- | Please see the README on GitHub at
--   <a>https://github.com/githubuser/ExchangeAlgebra#readme</a>
@package exchangealgebra
@version 0.4.0.0


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping system.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
--   
--   _Note_ : The current version 0.1.0.0 will be completely changed
--   shortly, especially in the accounts settings section.
module ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element

-- | Name :: Name of an item
type Name = Text

-- | Element Class: a type must be an instance of this class to serve as a
--   component of a basis.
--   
--   Each component of a basis must be an instance of this type class. It
--   provides wildcard-based pattern matching, enabling flexible basis
--   specification in transfer transformations and projections.
class (Eq a, Ord a, Show a, Hashable a, Typeable a) => Element a

-- | The wildcard value. Used for pattern matching in search, transfer
--   transformation, etc.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
wiledcard :: Element a => a

-- | Determines whether the element itself or any of its internal
--   components contains a wildcard. For tuple elements, returns True if
--   any component is a wildcard.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k) (k is the number of tuple components; O(1) for
--   primitive types)
haveWiledcard :: Element a => a -> Bool

-- | Determines whether the value is exactly the wildcard.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
isWiledcard :: Element a => a -> Bool

-- | Wildcard-ignoring transformation. If <tt>after</tt> is a wildcard,
--   returns <tt>before</tt>. Used inside transfer to fill wildcard
--   positions in the target basis with the original values.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k) (k is the number of tuple components; O(1) for
--   primitive types)
ignoreWiledcard :: Element a => a -> a -> a

-- | Wildcard-aware equality test. Returns True if either operand is a
--   wildcard.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k) (k is the number of tuple components; O(1) for
--   primitive types)
equal :: Element a => a -> a -> Bool

-- | Equality operator that treats wildcards as equal. Unlike <a>==</a>,
--   <tt>(.==)</tt> matches tuples that partially contain wildcards.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k) (k is the number of tuple components; O(1) for
--   primitive types)
(.==) :: Element a => a -> a -> Bool

-- | Inequality operator that treats wildcards as equal. Negation of
--   <tt>(.==)</tt>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k)
(./=) :: Element a => a -> a -> Bool

-- | Wildcard-aware comparison. Returns EQ if the two values are equal
--   under <tt>(.==)</tt>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k)
compareElement :: Element a => a -> a -> Ordering

-- | Wildcard-aware less-than comparison.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k)
(.<) :: Element a => a -> a -> Bool

-- | Wildcard-aware greater-than comparison.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k)
(.>) :: Element a => a -> a -> Bool

-- | Wildcard-aware less-than-or-equal comparison.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k)
(.<=) :: Element a => a -> a -> Bool

-- | Wildcard-aware greater-than-or-equal comparison.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k)
(.>=) :: Element a => a -> a -> Bool

-- | Wildcard-aware maximum.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k)
maxElement :: Element a => a -> a -> a

-- | Wildcard-aware minimum.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k)
minElement :: Element a => a -> a -> a
infix 4 .==
infix 4 ./=

-- | An existential type that holds each axis of a basis with its type
--   erased. Used to decompose multi-dimensional bases (tuples) into
--   per-axis keys for indexing.
data AxisKey
AxisKey :: !a -> AxisKey

-- | A type class for decomposing a basis element into a list of per-axis
--   <a>AxisKey</a>s. Overlapping instances are defined for tuple types so
--   that each component is decomposed into a separate <a>AxisKey</a>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(k) (k is the number of tuple components)
class (Element a) => AxisDecompose a
toAxisKeys :: AxisDecompose a => a -> [AxisKey]

-- | The current version 0.1.0.0 will be completely changed shortly,
--   especially this section.
data AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Cash
Cash :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Savings deposits
Deposits :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Current deposits
CurrentDeposits :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Securities
Securities :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Investment securities
InvestmentSecurities :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Long-term national bonds
LongTermNationalBonds :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Short-term national bonds
ShortTermNationalBonds :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Products
Products :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Machinery and equipment
Machinery :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Real estate
Building :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Vehicles
Vehicle :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Stock investment
StockInvestment :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Equipment investment
EquipmentInvestment :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Loans receivable
LongTermLoansReceivable :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Accounts receivable
AccountsReceivable :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Short-term loans receivable
ShortTermLoansReceivable :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Reserve deposit receivable
ReserveDepositReceivable :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Gold
Gold :: AccountTitles

-- | Asset: Government service expenditure
GovernmentService :: AccountTitles

-- | Equity: Capital stock
CapitalStock :: AccountTitles

-- | Equity: Retained earnings
RetainedEarnings :: AccountTitles

-- | Liability: Long-term loans payable
LongTermLoansPayable :: AccountTitles

-- | Liability: Short-term loans payable
ShortTermLoansPayable :: AccountTitles

-- | Liability: Loans payable
LoansPayable :: AccountTitles

-- | Liability: Reserve for depreciation
ReserveForDepreciation :: AccountTitles

-- | Liability: Deposits received
DepositPayable :: AccountTitles

-- | Liability: Long-term national bonds payable
LongTermNationalBondsPayable :: AccountTitles

-- | Liability: Short-term national bonds payable
ShortTermNationalBondsPayable :: AccountTitles

-- | Liability: Accounts payable
ReserveDepositPayable :: AccountTitles

-- | Liability: Central bank notes payable
CentralBankNotePayable :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Depreciation
Depreciation :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Cost of sales
SalesCost :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Travel and transportation
BusinessTrip :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Communication
Commutation :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Utilities
UtilitiesExpense :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Rent
RentExpense :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Advertising
AdvertisingExpense :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Delivery
DeliveryExpenses :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Supplies
SuppliesExpenses :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Miscellaneous
MiscellaneousExpenses :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Wages
WageExpenditure :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Interest expense
InterestExpense :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Taxes
TaxesExpense :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Consumables
ConsumptionExpenditure :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Subsidy expenditure
SubsidyExpense :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense
CentralBankPaymentExpense :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Purchases
Purchases :: AccountTitles

-- | Expense: Net income
NetIncome :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Value added
ValueAdded :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Subsidy income
SubsidyIncome :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: National bond interest earned
NationalBondInterestEarned :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Deposit interest earned
DepositInterestEarned :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Gross profit
GrossProfit :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Ordinary profit
OrdinaryProfit :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Interest earned
InterestEarned :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Receipt fee
ReceiptFee :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Rental income
RentalIncome :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Wage income
WageEarned :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Tax revenue
TaxesRevenue :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue
CentralBankPaymentIncome :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Sales
Sales :: AccountTitles

-- | Revenue: Net loss
NetLoss :: AccountTitles

-- | Wildcard
AccountTitle :: AccountTitles

-- | Subject of an account title
type Subject = Text

-- | Currency unit or physical quantity
data CountUnit
Yen :: CountUnit
Dollar :: CountUnit
Euro :: CountUnit
CNY :: CountUnit
Amount :: CountUnit
CountUnit :: CountUnit

-- | Shorthand notation for the wildcard. An alias for <tt>wiledcard</tt>.
--   Write <tt>(.#)</tt> when specifying patterns in projections and
--   transfer transformations.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
(.#) :: Element a => a
axisIsWildcard :: AxisKey -> Bool

-- | Representable types of kind <tt>*</tt>. This class is derivable in GHC
--   with the <tt>DeriveGeneric</tt> flag on.
--   
--   A <a>Generic</a> instance must satisfy the following laws:
--   
--   <pre>
--   <a>from</a> . <a>to</a> ≡ <a>id</a>
--   <a>to</a> . <a>from</a> ≡ <a>id</a>
--   </pre>
class () => Generic a
instance GHC.Generics.Generic ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles
instance GHC.Enum.Enum ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles
instance GHC.Classes.Ord ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles
instance GHC.Show.Show ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles
instance GHC.Generics.Generic ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit
instance GHC.Enum.Enum ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit

instance GHC.Classes.Ord ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit

instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit
instance Data.Binary.Class.Binary ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles
instance Data.Binary.Class.Binary ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element a => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AxisDecompose a
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element a, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element b) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AxisDecompose (a, b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element a, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element b, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element c) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AxisDecompose (a, b, c)

instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element a, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element b, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element c, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element d, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element f, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element g) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AxisDecompose (a, b, c, d, e, f, g)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AxisKey
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AxisKey
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element Data.Text.Internal.Text

instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element a, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element b, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element c, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element d) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element (a, b, c, d)

instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable Data.Time.LocalTime.Internal.TimeOfDay.TimeOfDay
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable Data.Time.Calendar.Days.Day


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping system.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
--   
--   _Note_ : The current version 0.1.0.0 will be completely changed
--   shortly, especially in the accounts settings section.
module ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base

-- | Type class for bases with a Hat component. Provides functionality to
--   decompose and compose a base into its Hat part and BasePart. Manages
--   the credit (Hat) / debit (Not) distinction at the base level in
--   exchange algebra.
class (BaseClass a, BaseClass (BasePart a), AxisDecompose (BasePart a)) => HatBaseClass a where {
    
    -- | The type of the base part excluding the Hat.
    type BasePart a;
}

-- | Extract the base part excluding the Hat. Complexity: O(1)
base :: (HatBaseClass a, BaseClass (BasePart a)) => a -> BasePart a

-- | Extract the Hat part. Complexity: O(1)
hat :: HatBaseClass a => a -> Hat

-- | Reconstruct a base from a Hat and a BasePart. Complexity: O(1)
merge :: HatBaseClass a => Hat -> BasePart a -> a

-- | Convert to the Hat side. Complexity: O(1)
toHat :: HatBaseClass a => a -> a

-- | Convert to the Not side. Complexity: O(1)
toNot :: HatBaseClass a => a -> a

-- | Reverse Hat/Not. Complexity: O(1)
revHat :: HatBaseClass a => a -> a

-- | Test whether the base is Hat. Complexity: O(1)
isHat :: HatBaseClass a => a -> Bool

-- | Test whether the base is Not. Complexity: O(1)
isNot :: HatBaseClass a => a -> Bool

-- | Compare bases with Hat. Defaults to <a>compareBase</a>. Complexity:
--   O(k)
compareHatBase :: HatBaseClass a => a -> a -> Ordering

-- | Base class definition. Any type that is an instance of this class
--   qualifies as a base.
class (Element a) => BaseClass a
compareBase :: BaseClass a => a -> a -> Ordering

-- | Hat definition
data Hat
Hat :: Hat
Not :: Hat
HatNot :: Hat
data BaseForSingleHat
BaseForSingleHat :: BaseForSingleHat

-- | Base with Hat. Attaches a Hat (credit-increase) / Not (debit-increase)
--   label to a base element such as an account title. Use the constructor
--   <tt>(:&lt;)</tt> as in <tt>Hat :&lt; Cash</tt>.
data HatBase a
[:<] :: BaseClass a => Hat -> a -> HatBase a

-- | Credit/Debit distinction. Credit is the credit side, Debit is the
--   debit side. Side is a wildcard.
data Side

-- | Credit side
Credit :: Side

-- | Debit side
Debit :: Side

-- | Wildcard
Side :: Side

-- | Fixed/Current distinction. Used for classifying account titles as
--   fixed or current.
data FixedCurrent

-- | Fixed
Fixed :: FixedCurrent

-- | Current
Current :: FixedCurrent

-- | Other (expenses, revenues, etc.)
Other :: FixedCurrent
data AccountDivision

-- | Assets
Assets :: AccountDivision

-- | Equity
Equity :: AccountDivision

-- | Liability
Liability :: AccountDivision

-- | Cost
Cost :: AccountDivision

-- | Revenue
Revenue :: AccountDivision

-- | BaseClass ⊃ HatBaseClass ⊃ ExBaseClass
--   
--   Extended type class for bases that carry an account title. Provides
--   access to and modification of account titles, account divisions, PIMO
--   classification, credit<i>debit determination, and fixed</i>current
--   classification.
class (HatBaseClass a) => ExBaseClass a

-- | Retrieve the account title from a base. Complexity: O(1)
getAccountTitle :: ExBaseClass a => a -> AccountTitles

-- | Change the account title of a base. Complexity: O(1)
setAccountTitle :: ExBaseClass a => a -> AccountTitles -> a

-- | Account title setter operator. An alias for <tt>setAccountTitle</tt>.
--   Complexity: O(1)
(.~) :: ExBaseClass a => a -> AccountTitles -> a

-- | Retrieve the account division
--   (Assets<i>Equity</i>Liability<i>Cost</i>Revenue). Complexity: O(1)
whatDiv :: ExBaseClass a => a -> AccountDivision

-- | Retrieve the PIMO classification (PS<i>IN</i>MS/OUT). Complexity: O(1)
whatPIMO :: ExBaseClass a => a -> PIMO

-- | Determine whether a base belongs to the Credit or Debit side. Takes
--   the Hat/Not reversal into account. Complexity: O(1)
whichSide :: ExBaseClass a => a -> Side

-- | Retrieve the fixed/current classification. Returns Current, Fixed, or
--   Other based on the account title.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
fixedCurrent :: ExBaseClass a => a -> FixedCurrent

-- | PIMO classification. Categories in exchange algebra: Product Stock
--   (PS), Income (IN), Money Stock (MS), and Outflow (OUT).
data PIMO

-- | Product Stock (Assets: production stock)
PS :: PIMO

-- | Income (Revenue: income flow)
IN :: PIMO

-- | Money Stock (Liability/Equity: monetary stock)
MS :: PIMO

-- | Outflow (Cost: expenditure flow)
OUT :: PIMO

-- | Type class for determining correspondences between account divisions.
--   Tests whether two account divisions form a pair in double-entry
--   bookkeeping (e.g., Assets <a>=</a> Liability).
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
class AccountBase a

-- | Test whether two account divisions are in a corresponding
--   relationship.
(<=>) :: AccountBase a => a -> a -> Bool
customError :: HasCallStack => String -> a

-- | Reverse the credit/debit side. Swaps Credit and Debit. The wildcard
--   Side is returned unchanged.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
switchSide :: Side -> Side

-- | Classify an account title into an account division
--   (Assets<i>Equity</i>Liability<i>Cost</i>Revenue).
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
classifyAccountDivision :: HasCallStack => AccountTitles -> AccountDivision
instance GHC.Generics.Generic ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance GHC.Show.Show ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance GHC.Classes.Ord ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance GHC.Enum.Enum ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance GHC.Generics.Generic ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseForSingleHat
instance GHC.Classes.Ord ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseForSingleHat
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseForSingleHat
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Side
instance GHC.Show.Show ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Side
instance GHC.Classes.Ord ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Side
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.FixedCurrent
instance GHC.Show.Show ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.FixedCurrent
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.AccountDivision
instance GHC.Show.Show ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.AccountDivision
instance GHC.Classes.Ord ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.AccountDivision
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.PIMO
instance GHC.Show.Show ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.PIMO
instance GHC.Classes.Ord ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.PIMO
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles)
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles, Data.Time.Calendar.Days.Day))
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Name))
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles))
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Name, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit))
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Name, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Subject))
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Name, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Subject, Data.Time.Calendar.Days.Day))
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Name, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Subject, Data.Time.Calendar.Days.Day, Data.Time.LocalTime.Internal.TimeOfDay.TimeOfDay))
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.AccountBase ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.PIMO
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.AccountBase ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.AccountDivision
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass a, Data.Binary.Class.Binary a) => Data.Binary.Class.Binary (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase a)
instance GHC.Show.Show (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase a)
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass a => Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase a)
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass a => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase a)
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass a => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase a)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass a, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AxisDecompose a) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBase a)
instance GHC.Show.Show ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseForSingleHat
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseForSingleHat
instance Data.Binary.Class.Binary ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseForSingleHat
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseForSingleHat
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseForSingleHat
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance Data.Binary.Class.Binary ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Hat
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e1, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e2) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass (e1, e2)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e1, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e2, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e3) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass (e1, e2, e3)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e1, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e2, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e3, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e4) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass (e1, e2, e3, e4)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e1, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e2, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e3, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e4, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e5) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass (e1, e2, e3, e4, e5)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e1, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e2, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e3, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e4, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e5, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Element e6) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass (e1, e2, e3, e4, e5, e6)
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.AccountTitles
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.Name
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.Element.CountUnit
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass Data.Time.Calendar.Days.Day
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BaseClass Data.Time.LocalTime.Internal.TimeOfDay.TimeOfDay


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping system.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
module ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra

-- | Type class providing approximate equality for numeric values. Performs
--   equality comparison with tolerance for floating-point rounding errors.
class (Eq a, Ord a) => Nearly a

-- | Complexity: O(1) Assumes primitive numeric operations and comparisons
--   are constant time.
isNearlyNum :: (Show a, Num a, Ord a) => a -> a -> a -> Bool

-- | Type class for Redundant Algebra. Provides fundamental exchange
--   algebra operations: hat, bar, norm, scalar product, and compress.
--   
--   Redundant ⊃ Exchange
--   
--   hat calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; (.^) (10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   10:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits) 10.00:<tt>Hat:&lt;Cash .+
--   10.00:</tt>Not:&lt;Deposits
--   
--   bar calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   10:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 5:<tt>Hat:&lt;Cash .+
--   5:</tt>Not:&lt;Deposits &gt;&gt;&gt; (.-) $ x .+ y
--   5.00:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+ 5.00:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits
--   
--   norm calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; norm $ 10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   10:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits 20.0
--   
--   (.*) calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; (.*) 5 $ 10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   10:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits 50.00:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   50.00:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits
--   
--   compress calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; compress $ 10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   5:</tt>Hat:&lt;Cash .+ 3:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash 5.00:</tt>Hat:&lt;Cash .+
--   13.00:@Not:&lt;Cash
class (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b, Monoid (a n b)) => Redundant a n b

-- | Hat operation. Flips Hat/Not on all elements. Complexity: O(1) for
--   singleton, O(n) for Liner (n is the number of base keys)
(.^) :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Bar operation. Cancels Hat/Not on the same base and retains only the
--   difference. Complexity: O(n) (n is the number of base keys)
(.-) :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Alias for bar operation. Identical to <tt>(.-)</tt>.
bar :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Aggregates values on the same base. Sums while preserving the Hat/Not
--   distinction. Complexity: O(n) (n is the number of base keys)
compress :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Addition of algebra elements. Alias for the Monoid <tt>&lt;&gt;</tt>
--   operation. Complexity: O(union cost)
(.+) :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b -> a n b

-- | Scalar product. Multiplies all element values by a scalar. Complexity:
--   O(1) for singleton, O(n) for Liner
(.*) :: Redundant a n b => n -> a n b -> a n b

-- | Norm. Returns the sum of all element values. Complexity: O(n) (n is
--   the number of base keys)
norm :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> n

-- | Addition in an Applicative context. Complexity: O(union cost)
(<+) :: (Redundant a n b, Applicative f) => f (a n b) -> f (a n b) -> f (a n b)
infixr 7 .^
infixr 2 .-
infixr 3 .+
infixr 3 <+

-- | Type class for Exchange Algebra. In addition to Redundant Algebra,
--   provides debit(R)<i>credit(L) decomposition, stock
--   increase(P)</i>decrease(M) decomposition, and balance checking.
class (Redundant a n b) => Exchange a n b

-- | Extracts only the debit side elements. Complexity: O(s)
decR :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Extracts only the credit side elements. Complexity: O(s)
decL :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Extracts only the Hat (stock increase) side elements. Complexity: O(s)
decP :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Extracts only the Not (stock decrease) side elements. Complexity: O(s)
decM :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Checks whether the norms of debit and credit sides are equal.
--   Complexity: O(s)
balance :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> Bool

-- | Returns the debit-credit difference as a (Side, difference) pair.
--   Complexity: O(s)
diffRL :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> (Side, n)

-- | Type class for algebra element values. Provides zero-value and
--   error-value predicates. Instances are defined for <a>Double</a> and
--   <a>Double</a> (non-negative reals).
class (Show n, Ord n, Eq n, Nearly n, Fractional n, RealFloat n, Num n) => HatVal n

-- | Zero value. Complexity: O(1)
zeroValue :: HatVal n => n

-- | Tests whether the value is zero. Complexity: O(1)
isZeroValue :: HatVal n => n -> Bool

-- | Tests whether the value is an error value (NaN, Infinity, etc.).
--   Complexity: O(1)
isErrorValue :: HatVal n => n -> Bool

-- | Algebra element. An element of exchange algebra consisting of a
--   value-base pair. Zero is the zero element, <tt>(:</tt>)@ is a
--   singleton, and Liner is a HashMap-based multi-element representation.
data Alg v b
[Zero] :: Alg v b
[:@] :: !v -> !b -> Alg v b
[Liner] :: !HashMap (BasePart b) (Pair v) -> ~IntMap (HashMap AxisKey IntSet) -> ~HashMap (BasePart b) Int -> ~IntMap (BasePart b) -> ~Int -> ~IntSet -> Alg v b
infixr 6 :@

-- | Tests whether the algebra element is zero (empty).
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
isZero :: Alg v b -> Bool

-- | Smart constructor that builds an algebra element from a value and a
--   base. Returns Zero for zero values, and throws an exception for error
--   values.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
(.@) :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => n -> b -> Alg n b
infixr 6 .@

-- | Constructs an algebra element in an Applicative context. Lifted
--   version of <tt>(.</tt>)@.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1) + Applicative effects
(<@) :: (HatVal n, Applicative f, HatBaseClass b) => f n -> b -> f (Alg n b)
infixr 6 <@

-- | Returns all values contained in the algebra element as a list.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
vals :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => Alg v b -> [v]

-- | Returns all bases contained in the algebra element as a list.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
bases :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => Alg v b -> [b]

-- | convert List to Alg n b Complexity: O(sum of <a>union</a> costs),
--   because this is implemented via <a>mconcat</a>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg NN.Double (HatBase AccountTitles)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; xs = [1:@Hat:&lt;Cash,1:@Not:&lt;Deposits, 2:@Hat:&lt;Cash, 2:@Not:&lt;Deposits] :: [Test]
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; fromList xs
--   1.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash .+ 1.00:@Not:&lt;Deposits .+ 2.00:@Not:&lt;Deposits
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg NN.Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1:@Not:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 2:@Not:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; fromList [x,y]
--   1.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1.00:@Not:&lt;Amount .+ 2.00:@Not:&lt;Amount
--   </pre>
fromList :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => [Alg v b] -> Alg v b

-- | Converts an algebra element to a list. Complexity: O(s) (s is the
--   total number of scalar entries)
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; toList (10:@Hat:&lt;(Cash) .+ 10:@Hat:&lt;(Deposits) .+ Zero :: Alg NN.Double (HatBase AccountTitles))
--   [10.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash,10.00:@Hat:&lt;Deposits]
--   </pre>
--   
--   you need define type variables to use this for Zero &gt;&gt;&gt;
--   toList Zero :: [Alg NN.Double (HatBase AccountTitles)] []
toList :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => Alg v b -> [Alg v b]

-- | Strict left fold over scalar entries without building an intermediate
--   list.
foldEntries :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => (acc -> v -> b -> acc) -> acc -> Alg v b -> acc

-- | Summation function that applies a function to each element of a list
--   and sums the results. Complexity: O(sum of <a>union</a> costs over
--   produced elements).
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg NN.Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; sigma [1,2] (\x -&gt; x:@Hat:&lt;Yen)
--   1.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen
--   </pre>
sigma :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => [a] -> (a -> Alg v b) -> Alg v b

-- | Conditional summation over a double loop. For all combinations of two
--   lists, applies the function only to pairs that satisfy the condition
--   and sums the results.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|xs| * |ys| * union cost)
sigma2When :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => [a] -> [c] -> (a -> c -> Bool) -> (a -> c -> Alg v b) -> Alg v b

-- | Summation using keys and values from a Map. Skips entries with zero
--   values.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|map| * union cost)
sigmaFromMap :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Ord k) => Map k v -> (k -> v -> Alg v b) -> Alg v b

-- | Complexity: O(s log s), dominated by sorting the list representation.
toASCList :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => Alg v b -> [Alg v b]

-- | map Complexity: O(s + c), where s is traversed scalar entries and c is
--   transformed output size.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1:@Not:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2:@Not:&lt;Yen .+ 2:@Hat:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; map (\ (x:@hb) -&gt;  x:@(toHat hb)) $ x .+ y
--   1.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1.00:@Hat:&lt;Amount .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Amount
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg Double Hat
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1:@Hat .+ 1:@Not :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2:@Not .+ 2:@Hat :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; map (\ (x:@hb) -&gt; (2 * x):@hb) $ x .+ y
--   2.00:@Hat .+ 4.00:@Hat .+ 2.00:@Not .+ 4.00:@Not
--   </pre>
map :: (HasCallStack, HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => (Alg v b -> Alg v b) -> Alg v b -> Alg v b

-- | filter Complexity: O(s), where s is total number of scalar entries.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1:@Not:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2:@Not:&lt;Yen .+ 2:@Hat:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; filter (isHat . _hatBase) $ x .+ y
--   1.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Amount
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1:@Not:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2:@Not:&lt;Yen .+ 2:@Hat:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; filter ((1 &lt;). _val) $ x .+ y
--   2.00:@Not:&lt;Yen .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Amount
--   </pre>
filter :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => (Alg v b -> Bool) -> Alg v b -> Alg v b

-- | proj Complexity: - exact single-key path: expected O(1) - wildcard
--   single-key path: O(queryAxisPosting + c * verify) - multi-pattern
--   path: O(sum pattern costs + union costs) where c is candidate count
--   returned by the posting index. &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg NN.Double
--   (HatBase CountUnit) &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1:<tt>Hat:&lt;Yen .+
--   1:</tt>Not:&lt;Amount :: Test &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2:<tt>Not:&lt;Yen .+
--   2:</tt>Hat:&lt;Amount :: Test &gt;&gt;&gt; proj [Hat:&lt;Yen] $ x .+ y
--   1.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg NN.Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1:@Not:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2:@Not:&lt;Yen .+ 2:@Hat:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; proj [HatNot:&lt;Amount] $ x .+ y
--   2.00:@Hat:&lt;Amount .+ 1.00:@Not:&lt;Amount
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg NN.Double (HatBase (AccountTitles, CountUnit))
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1:@Hat:&lt;(Cash,Yen) .+ 1:@Not:&lt;(Products,Amount) :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2:@Not:&lt;(Cash,Yen) .+ 2:@Hat:&lt;(Deposits,Yen) :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; proj [Hat:&lt;((.#),Yen)] $ x .+ y
--   1.00:@Hat:&lt;(Cash,Yen) .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;(Deposits,Yen)
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = HatBase CountUnit
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; compareHatBase (Not:&lt;(.#) :: Test) (Not:&lt;Yen :: Test)
--   EQ
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg NN.Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1:@Not:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2:@Not:&lt;Yen .+ 2:@Hat:&lt;Amount :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; proj [Not:&lt;(.#)] $ x .+ y
--   2.00:@Not:&lt;Yen .+ 1.00:@Not:&lt;Amount
--   </pre>
proj :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => [b] -> Alg v b -> Alg v b

-- | Projects only the credit side elements. Use this instead of decL when
--   the base contains non-Enum elements such as Text or Int.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
projCredit :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Projects only the debit side elements. Use this instead of decR when
--   the base contains non-Enum elements such as Text or Int.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
projDebit :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Projects only the elements matching the specified account title.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
projByAccountTitle :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => AccountTitles -> Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Complexity: O(cost(proj) + cost(bar) + cost(norm)).
projNorm :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => [b] -> Alg n b -> n

-- | Compute the net balance as the difference of two projections.
--   <tt>balanceBy plusBases minusBases alg</tt> computes <tt>projNorm
--   plusBases alg - projNorm minusBases alg</tt>.
--   
--   Useful for calculating stock quantities, profits, etc.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type T = Alg Double (HatBase AccountTitles)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; let alg = 100 :@ Not:&lt;Cash .+ 30 :@ Hat:&lt;Cash :: T
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; balanceBy [Not:&lt;Cash] [Hat:&lt;Cash] alg
--   70.0
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; balanceBy [Hat:&lt;Cash] [Not:&lt;Cash] alg
--   -70.0
--   </pre>
balanceBy :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => [b] -> [b] -> Alg n b -> n

-- | Fold algebra entries into a <tt>Map</tt>, combining values with
--   <tt>(+)</tt>.
--   
--   The selector function examines each entry <tt>(v, b)</tt> and
--   optionally returns a <tt>(key, value)</tt> pair. Values for duplicate
--   keys are summed.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type T = Alg Double (HatBase AccountTitles)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; let alg = 10 :@ Hat:&lt;Cash .+ 20 :@ Hat:&lt;Deposits .+ 5 :@ Hat:&lt;Cash :: T
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; let f v (Hat :&lt; a) = Just (a, v); f _ _ = Nothing
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; foldEntriesToMap f alg
--   fromList [(Cash,15.0),(Deposits,20.0)]
--   </pre>
foldEntriesToMap :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Ord k) => (v -> b -> Maybe (k, v)) -> Alg v b -> Map k v

-- | Projects only current assets. Extracts asset items classified as
--   current from the debit side.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
projCurrentAssets :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Projects only fixed assets. Extracts asset items classified as fixed
--   from the debit side.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
projFixedAssets :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Projects only deferred assets. Tax-specific deferred assets are
--   presented under "investments and other assets" with appropriate items
--   such as long-term prepaid expenses.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
projDeferredAssets :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Projects only current liabilities. Extracts liability items classified
--   as current from the credit side.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
projCurrentLiability :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Projects only fixed liabilities. Extracts liability items classified
--   as fixed from the credit side.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s is the total number of scalar entries)
projFixedLiability :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Projects only capital stock.
--   
--   <b>Note</b>: Not yet implemented. Calling this will throw an
--   exception.
projCapitalStock :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Rounding (ceiling). Applied to the results of division and
--   multiplication; uses ceiling rounding by default. This should be
--   applied to all multiplication and division of account titles.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
rounding :: Double -> Double

-- | Merge multiple Algs by directly combining their internal HashMaps,
--   building the AxisPosting index only once at the end.
unionsMerge :: (HatVal n, Foldable f, HatBaseClass b) => f (Alg n b) -> Alg n b
instance GHC.Classes.Eq v => GHC.Classes.Eq (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Pair v)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b, Data.Binary.Class.Binary v, Data.Binary.Class.Binary b, Data.Binary.Class.Binary (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.BasePart b)) => Data.Binary.Class.Binary (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg v b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b) => GHC.Classes.Eq (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg v b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b) => GHC.Classes.Ord (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg v b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b) => GHC.Show.Show (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg v b)
instance Control.DeepSeq.NFData (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg v b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal n, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b) => GHC.Base.Semigroup (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg n b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal n, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b) => GHC.Base.Monoid (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg n b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal n, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Redundant ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg n b
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal n, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass b) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Exchange ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg n b
instance Data.Binary.Class.Binary v => Data.Binary.Class.Binary (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Pair v)
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v => GHC.Classes.Ord (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Pair v)
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal Numeric.NonNegative.Wrapper.Double
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal GHC.Types.Double
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Nearly GHC.Types.Int
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Nearly GHC.Num.Integer.Integer
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Nearly GHC.Types.Float
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Nearly GHC.Types.Double
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Nearly Numeric.NonNegative.Wrapper.Double
instance GHC.Float.RealFloat Numeric.NonNegative.Wrapper.Double


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping systems.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
--   
--   _Note_ : The current version 0.1.0.0 will be completely changed
--   shortly, especially in the accounts settings section.
module ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Transfer

-- | Transfer transformation table
data TransTable n b
[NullTable] :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => TransTable n b
[TransTable] :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => Size -> b -> (n -> n) -> b -> TransTable n b -> TransTable n b -> TransTable n b

-- | Tests whether the transfer table is empty.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
isNullTable :: TransTable n b -> Bool
transfer :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> TransTable n b -> Alg n b

-- | make TransTable from list same as fromList &gt;&gt;&gt; table $
--   Hat:<a>:-</a> Hat:<a>|% (id :: NN.Double -</a> NN.Double) ++
--   Hat:<a>:-</a> Hat:&lt;(Cash) |% id
--   [(Hat:<a>Cash,Hat:&lt;Building,&lt;function</a>),(Hat:<a>Building,Hat:&lt;Cash,&lt;function</a>)]
table :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => [(b, b, n -> n)] -> TransTable n b

-- | A part of a transfer rule. Represents a source-to-target base pair in
--   <tt>from :-&gt; to</tt> form.
data TransTableParts b

-- | Transfer rule construction operator. <tt>from .-&gt; to</tt> produces
--   a <a>TransTableParts</a>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
(.->) :: HatBaseClass b => b -> b -> TransTableParts b
infixr 8 .->

-- | Syntax to make list for makeList
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; Hat:&lt;(Yen,Cash):-&gt; Hat:&lt;(Yen,Building) |% (id :: NN.Double -&gt; NN.Double) ++ Not:&lt;(Yen,Building)  :-&gt; Not:&lt;(Yen, Cash)  |% id
--   [(Hat:&lt;(Yen,Cash),Hat:&lt;(Yen,Building),&lt;function&gt;),(Not:&lt;(Yen,Building),Not:&lt;(Yen,Cash),&lt;function&gt;)]
--   </pre>
(|%) :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => TransTableParts b -> (n -> n) -> [(b, b, n -> n)]
infixr 7 |%

-- | Build an indexed fast transfer function from a list of transfer rules.
--   More efficient than <tt>transfer</tt> when repeatedly applying the
--   same TransTable.
--   
--   Complexity: Build O(r log r) (r = number of rules); apply O(s) (s =
--   number of entries)
createTransfer :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => [(b, b, n -> n)] -> Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Income Summary Account: compute net income for the current period.
incomeSummaryAccount :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Net income transfer. Transfers NetIncome/NetLoss to RetainedEarnings.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s = total number of scalar entries)
netIncomeTransfer :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Transfer to Gross Profit. Consolidates Sales, Purchases,
--   WageExpenditure, Depreciation, and ValueAdded into GrossProfit.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s = total number of scalar entries)
grossProfitTransfer :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Ordinary Profit Transfer
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Alg Double (HatBase (CountUnit, AccountTitles))
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 2279.0:@Not:&lt;(Yen,Depreciation) .+ 500475.0:@Not:&lt;(Yen,InterestEarned) :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; ordinaryProfitTransfer x
--   2279.00:@Hat:&lt;(Yen,OrdinaryProfit) .+ 500475.00:@Not:&lt;(Yen,OrdinaryProfit)
--   </pre>
ordinaryProfitTransfer :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Transfer to Retained Earnings. Transfers OrdinaryProfit to
--   RetainedEarnings.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s = total number of scalar entries)
retainedEarningTransfer :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Internal step of the final stock transfer from income statement to
--   retained earnings. Cost accounts are transferred to RetainedEarnings
--   with Hat/Not flipped; revenue accounts are transferred to
--   RetainedEarnings as-is.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s = total number of scalar entries)
finalStockTransferStep :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b

-- | Final Stock Transfer (closing entries). Transfers all cost and revenue
--   accounts to RetainedEarnings and cancels via the bar operation.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s = total number of scalar entries)
finalStockTransfer :: (HatVal n, ExBaseClass b) => Alg n b -> Alg n b
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b => GHC.Show.Show (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Transfer.TransTableParts b)
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b => GHC.Show.Show (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Transfer.TransTable n b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal n, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b) => GHC.Base.Semigroup (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Transfer.TransTable n b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal n, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b) => GHC.Base.Monoid (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Transfer.TransTable n b)
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal n => GHC.Show.Show (n -> n)


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping system.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
module ExchangeAlgebra.Journal

-- | Type class for algebra element values. Provides zero-value and
--   error-value predicates. Instances are defined for <a>Double</a> and
--   <a>Double</a> (non-negative reals).
class (Show n, Ord n, Eq n, Nearly n, Fractional n, RealFloat n, Num n) => HatVal n

-- | Zero value. Complexity: O(1)
zeroValue :: HatVal n => n

-- | Tests whether the value is zero. Complexity: O(1)
isZeroValue :: HatVal n => n -> Bool

-- | Tests whether the value is an error value (NaN, Infinity, etc.).
--   Complexity: O(1)
isErrorValue :: HatVal n => n -> Bool

-- | Type class for bases with a Hat component. Provides functionality to
--   decompose and compose a base into its Hat part and BasePart. Manages
--   the credit (Hat) / debit (Not) distinction at the base level in
--   exchange algebra.
class (BaseClass a, BaseClass (BasePart a), AxisDecompose (BasePart a)) => HatBaseClass a where {
    
    -- | The type of the base part excluding the Hat.
    type BasePart a;
}

-- | Extract the base part excluding the Hat. Complexity: O(1)
base :: (HatBaseClass a, BaseClass (BasePart a)) => a -> BasePart a

-- | Extract the Hat part. Complexity: O(1)
hat :: HatBaseClass a => a -> Hat

-- | Reconstruct a base from a Hat and a BasePart. Complexity: O(1)
merge :: HatBaseClass a => Hat -> BasePart a -> a

-- | Convert to the Hat side. Complexity: O(1)
toHat :: HatBaseClass a => a -> a

-- | Convert to the Not side. Complexity: O(1)
toNot :: HatBaseClass a => a -> a

-- | Reverse Hat/Not. Complexity: O(1)
revHat :: HatBaseClass a => a -> a

-- | Test whether the base is Hat. Complexity: O(1)
isHat :: HatBaseClass a => a -> Bool

-- | Test whether the base is Not. Complexity: O(1)
isNot :: HatBaseClass a => a -> Bool

-- | Compare bases with Hat. Defaults to <a>compareBase</a>. Complexity:
--   O(k)
compareHatBase :: HatBaseClass a => a -> a -> Ordering

-- | Type class for Redundant Algebra. Provides fundamental exchange
--   algebra operations: hat, bar, norm, scalar product, and compress.
--   
--   Redundant ⊃ Exchange
--   
--   hat calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; (.^) (10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   10:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits) 10.00:<tt>Hat:&lt;Cash .+
--   10.00:</tt>Not:&lt;Deposits
--   
--   bar calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   10:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 5:<tt>Hat:&lt;Cash .+
--   5:</tt>Not:&lt;Deposits &gt;&gt;&gt; (.-) $ x .+ y
--   5.00:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+ 5.00:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits
--   
--   norm calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; norm $ 10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   10:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits 20.0
--   
--   (.*) calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; (.*) 5 $ 10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   10:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits 50.00:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   50.00:</tt>Hat:&lt;Deposits
--   
--   compress calculation &gt;&gt;&gt; compress $ 10:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash .+
--   5:</tt>Hat:&lt;Cash .+ 3:<tt>Not:&lt;Cash 5.00:</tt>Hat:&lt;Cash .+
--   13.00:@Not:&lt;Cash
class (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b, Monoid (a n b)) => Redundant a n b

-- | Hat operation. Flips Hat/Not on all elements. Complexity: O(1) for
--   singleton, O(n) for Liner (n is the number of base keys)
(.^) :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Bar operation. Cancels Hat/Not on the same base and retains only the
--   difference. Complexity: O(n) (n is the number of base keys)
(.-) :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Alias for bar operation. Identical to <tt>(.-)</tt>.
bar :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Aggregates values on the same base. Sums while preserving the Hat/Not
--   distinction. Complexity: O(n) (n is the number of base keys)
compress :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Addition of algebra elements. Alias for the Monoid <tt>&lt;&gt;</tt>
--   operation. Complexity: O(union cost)
(.+) :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> a n b -> a n b

-- | Scalar product. Multiplies all element values by a scalar. Complexity:
--   O(1) for singleton, O(n) for Liner
(.*) :: Redundant a n b => n -> a n b -> a n b

-- | Norm. Returns the sum of all element values. Complexity: O(n) (n is
--   the number of base keys)
norm :: Redundant a n b => a n b -> n

-- | Addition in an Applicative context. Complexity: O(union cost)
(<+) :: (Redundant a n b, Applicative f) => f (a n b) -> f (a n b) -> f (a n b)
infixr 7 .^
infixr 2 .-
infixr 3 .+
infixr 3 <+

-- | Type class for Exchange Algebra. In addition to Redundant Algebra,
--   provides debit(R)<i>credit(L) decomposition, stock
--   increase(P)</i>decrease(M) decomposition, and balance checking.
class (Redundant a n b) => Exchange a n b

-- | Extracts only the debit side elements. Complexity: O(s)
decR :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Extracts only the credit side elements. Complexity: O(s)
decL :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Extracts only the Hat (stock increase) side elements. Complexity: O(s)
decP :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Extracts only the Not (stock decrease) side elements. Complexity: O(s)
decM :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> a n b

-- | Checks whether the norms of debit and credit sides are equal.
--   Complexity: O(s)
balance :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> Bool

-- | Returns the debit-credit difference as a (Side, difference) pair.
--   Complexity: O(s)
diffRL :: Exchange a n b => a n b -> (Side, n)
pattern (:@) :: () => !v -> !b -> Alg v b
infixr 6 :@

-- | Smart constructor that builds an algebra element from a value and a
--   base. Returns Zero for zero values, and throws an exception for error
--   values.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
(.@) :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => n -> b -> Alg n b
infixr 6 .@

-- | Type class for journal annotations (notes attached to postings).
--   <tt>plank</tt> represents a blank note (analogous to <tt>mempty</tt>
--   in <tt>Monoid</tt>).
--   
--   <tt>toNoteAxisKeys</tt> decomposes a Note into per-axis keys for
--   AxisPosting indexing, mirroring how <tt>toAxisKeys</tt> works for
--   basis elements. For tuple Note types, each component becomes a
--   separate axis. The default returns a single axis containing the Note
--   itself.
class (Show a, Eq a, Ord a, Hashable a, Typeable a) => Note a
plank :: Note a => a
isPlank :: Note a => a -> Bool

-- | Decompose a Note into per-axis keys for AxisPosting indexing. Default:
--   single axis with the Note itself.
toNoteAxisKeys :: Note a => a -> [NoteAxisKey]

-- | An existential type that holds each axis of a Note with its type
--   erased. Used to decompose multi-dimensional Note types (tuples) into
--   per-axis keys for indexing, mirroring how <a>AxisKey</a> works for
--   basis elements in <a>Alg</a>.
data NoteAxisKey
NoteAxisKey :: !a -> NoteAxisKey

-- | Transaction data with annotations. Stored in a base + delta two-layer
--   structure with per-axis indices. Base index is lazy (built on first
--   axis query), while delta index is updated incrementally. Updates are
--   appended only to delta and periodically compacted into base.
data Journal n v b
[Journal] :: (Note n, HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => !HashMap n (Alg v b) -> !HashMap n (Alg v b) -> !Int -> NoteAxisPosting n -> !NoteAxisPosting n -> Journal n v b
pattern Zero :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => Journal n v b

-- | Smart constructor that attaches a Note (annotation) to an algebra
--   element to build a Journal.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
(.|) :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => Alg v b -> n -> Journal n v b
infixr 2 .|

-- | Combine entries from all Notes in a Journal into a single Alg.
--   
--   Complexity: O(total number of base keys across all Notes)
toAlg :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => Journal n v b -> Alg v b

-- | Retrieve all entries of a Journal as a HashMap. Merges the base and
--   delta layers.
--   
--   Complexity: O(n) where n is the number of Notes
toMap :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => Journal n v b -> HashMap n (Alg v b)

-- | Construct a Journal from a HashMap.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1). Base axis index is built lazily on first axis query.
fromMap :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => HashMap n (Alg v b) -> Journal n v b

-- | fromList
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Journal String Double (HatBase AccountTitles)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = [(1.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash .| z) | z &lt;- ["Loan Payment","Purchace Apple"]] :: [Test]
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; fromList x
--   1.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash.|"Loan Payment" .+ 1.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash.|"Purchace Apple"
--   </pre>
fromList :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => [Journal n v b] -> Journal n v b

-- | Summation function that applies a function to each list element and
--   sums the resulting Journals.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|xs| * union cost)
sigma :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => [a] -> (a -> Journal n v b) -> Journal n v b

-- | Conditional summation over a double loop (Journal version). Applies
--   the function only to pairs that satisfy the condition across all
--   combinations of two lists, and sums the results.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|xs| * |ys| * union cost)
sigma2When :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => [a] -> [c] -> (a -> c -> Bool) -> (a -> c -> Journal n v b) -> Journal n v b

-- | Sum each list element as an Alg on the specified Note and store the
--   result in a Journal. Returns an empty Journal if the result is zero.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|xs| * union cost)
sigmaOn :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => n -> [a] -> (a -> Alg v b) -> Journal n v b

-- | Sum Alg values from Map keys and values on the specified Note and
--   store the result in a Journal. Map version of <a>sigmaOn</a>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|map| * union cost)
sigmaOnFromMap :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n, Ord k) => n -> Map k v -> (k -> v -> Alg v b) -> Journal n v b

-- | Summation in a monadic context. Applies a monadic function to each
--   element and mconcats the results.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|xs| * cost(f))
sigmaM :: (Monoid m, Monad m0) => [a] -> (a -> m0 m) -> m0 m

-- | Apply function f to the entry of each Note in the Journal. Applies to
--   merged Note entries (base + delta), preserving semantics.
--   
--   Complexity: O(j * cost(f)) where j is the number of Notes
map :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => (Alg v b -> Alg v b) -> Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | Insert x into y. If x's Note already exists in y, it is overwritten
--   with x's value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Journal String Double (HatBase AccountTitles)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 10.00:@Not:&lt;Cash .| "A" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 20.00:@Not:&lt;Cash .| "B" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; z = 30.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash .| "A" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; insert z (x .+ y)
--   30.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash.|"A" .+ 20.00:@Not:&lt;Cash.|"B"
--   </pre>
insert :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => Journal n v b -> Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | projWithNote Projecting with Note.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Journal String Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1.00:@Not:&lt;Amount .| "cat"  :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 2.00:@Not:&lt;Amount .| "dog"  :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; z = 3.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 3.00:@Not:&lt;Amount .| "fish" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; projWithNote ["dog","cat"] (x .+ y .+ z)
--   2.00:@Not:&lt;Amount.|"dog" .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen.|"dog" .+ 1.00:@Not:&lt;Amount.|"cat" .+ 1.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen.|"cat"
--   </pre>
projWithNote :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => [n] -> Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | projWithBase Projecting with Base.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Journal String Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1.00:@Not:&lt;Amount .| "cat"  :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2.00:@Not:&lt;Yen .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Amount .| "dog"  :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; z = 3.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 3.00:@Not:&lt;Amount .| "fish" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; projWithBase [Not:&lt;Amount] (x .+ y .+ z)
--   3.00:@Not:&lt;Amount.|"fish" .+ 1.00:@Not:&lt;Amount.|"cat"
--   </pre>
projWithBase :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => [b] -> Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | projWithNoteBase Projecting with Note and Base.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Journal String Double (HatBase CountUnit)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 1.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 1.00:@Not:&lt;Amount .| "cat"  :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 2.00:@Not:&lt;Yen .+ 2.00:@Hat:&lt;Amount .| "dog"  :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; z = 3.00:@Hat:&lt;Yen .+ 3.00:@Not:&lt;Amount .| "fish" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; projWithNoteBase ["dog","fish"] [Not:&lt;Amount] (x .+ y .+ z)
--   3.00:@Not:&lt;Amount.|"fish"
--   </pre>
projWithNoteBase :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => [n] -> [b] -> Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | Directly compute the norm after filtering by the specified bases.
--   Equivalent to <tt>norm (projWithBase bs js)</tt> but without
--   constructing an intermediate Journal.
--   
--   Complexity: O(j * proj cost) where j is the number of Notes
projWithBaseNorm :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => [b] -> Journal n v b -> v

-- | Directly compute the norm after filtering by the specified Notes and
--   bases. Equivalent to <tt>norm (projWithNoteBase ns bs js)</tt> but
--   without constructing an intermediate Journal.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|ns| * proj cost)
projWithNoteNorm :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => [n] -> [b] -> Journal n v b -> v

-- | Filter by a predicate on Note-entry pairs. Applies the filter to both
--   the base and delta layers.
--   
--   Complexity: O(n) where n is the number of Notes
filterWithNote :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => (n -> Alg v b -> Bool) -> Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | Efficiently filter a Journal to entries whose Note matches on the
--   specified axis. Uses base/delta NoteAxisPosting indices for
--   O(|result|) retrieval after index construction.
--   
--   Axis numbers are 0-indexed. For a Note type <tt>(EventName,
--   Term)</tt>:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>axis 0 corresponds to EventName</li>
--   <li>axis 1 corresponds to Term</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   For non-tuple Note types, axis 0 is the only valid axis.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|result|) after index construction; O(n) for first
--   base-axis query on a Journal value
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Journal (String, Int) Double (HatBase AccountTitles)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 10.00:@Not:&lt;Cash .| ("A", 1) :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 20.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash .| ("B", 1) :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; z = 30.00:@Not:&lt;Cash .| ("A", 2) :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; filterByAxis 0 (NoteAxisKey "A") (x .+ y .+ z)
--   30.00:@Not:&lt;Cash.|("A",2) .+ 10.00:@Not:&lt;Cash.|("A",1)
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; filterByAxis 1 (NoteAxisKey (1 :: Int)) (x .+ y .+ z)
--   20.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash.|("B",1) .+ 10.00:@Not:&lt;Cash.|("A",1)
--   </pre>
filterByAxis :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => Int -> NoteAxisKey -> Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | gather Gathers all Alg into one on the given Note.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Journal String Double (EA.HatBase EA.AccountTitles)
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 20.00:@Not:&lt;Cash .+ 20.00:@Hat:&lt;Deposits .| "Withdrawal" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 10.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash .+ 10.00:@Not:&lt;Deposits .| "Deposits" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; gather "A" (x .+ y)
--   10.00:@Not:&lt;Deposits.|"A" .+ 20.00:@Hat:&lt;Deposits.|"A" .+ 20.00:@Not:&lt;Cash.|"A" .+ 10.00:@Hat:&lt;Cash.|"A"
--   </pre>
gather :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => n -> Journal n v b -> Journal n v b
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note n, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b, Data.Binary.Class.Binary n, Data.Binary.Class.Binary (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Alg v b)) => Data.Binary.Class.Binary (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Journal n v b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note n) => GHC.Show.Show (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Journal n v b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note n) => GHC.Base.Semigroup (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Journal n v b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note n) => GHC.Base.Monoid (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Journal n v b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.HatBaseClass b, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note n) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Redundant (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Journal n) v b
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note n, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.HatVal v, ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Base.ExBaseClass b) => ExchangeAlgebra.Algebra.Exchange (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Journal n) v b
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note GHC.Types.Int
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note GHC.Base.String
instance ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note Data.Text.Internal.Text
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note a, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note b) => ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note (a, b)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note a, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note b, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note c) => ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note (a, b, c)
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note a, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note b, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note c, ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note d) => ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Note (a, b, c, d)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.NoteAxisKey
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.NoteAxisKey


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping system.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
--   
--   _Note_ : The current version 0.1.0.0 will be completely changed
--   shortly, especially in the accounts settings section.
module ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Transfer

-- | Transfer transformation table
data TransTable n b
[NullTable] :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => TransTable n b
[TransTable] :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => Size -> b -> (n -> n) -> b -> TransTable n b -> TransTable n b -> TransTable n b

-- | Tests whether the transfer table is empty.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
isNullTable :: TransTable n b -> Bool

-- | make TransTable from list same as fromList &gt;&gt;&gt; table $
--   Hat:<a>:-</a> Hat:<a>|% (id :: NN.Double -</a> NN.Double) ++
--   Hat:<a>:-</a> Hat:&lt;(Cash) |% id
--   [(Hat:<a>Cash,Hat:&lt;Building,&lt;function</a>),(Hat:<a>Building,Hat:&lt;Cash,&lt;function</a>)]
table :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => [(b, b, n -> n)] -> TransTable n b

-- | A part of a transfer rule. Represents a source-to-target base pair in
--   <tt>from :-&gt; to</tt> form.
data TransTableParts b

-- | Transfer rule construction operator. <tt>from .-&gt; to</tt> produces
--   a <a>TransTableParts</a>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
(.->) :: HatBaseClass b => b -> b -> TransTableParts b
infixr 8 .->

-- | Syntax to make list for makeList
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; Hat:&lt;(Yen,Cash):-&gt; Hat:&lt;(Yen,Building) |% (id :: NN.Double -&gt; NN.Double) ++ Not:&lt;(Yen,Building)  :-&gt; Not:&lt;(Yen, Cash)  |% id
--   [(Hat:&lt;(Yen,Cash),Hat:&lt;(Yen,Building),&lt;function&gt;),(Not:&lt;(Yen,Building),Not:&lt;(Yen,Cash),&lt;function&gt;)]
--   </pre>
(|%) :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b) => TransTableParts b -> (n -> n) -> [(b, b, n -> n)]
infixr 7 |%

-- | Apply transfer transformations to each Note entry in a Journal.
--   Wildcard portions within tuples are not transformed and retain their
--   original values.
--   
--   Complexity: O(j * s) (j = number of Notes, s = number of scalar
--   entries per Note)
transfer :: (HatVal v, HatBaseClass b, Note n) => Journal n v b -> TransTable v b -> Journal n v b

-- | Compute net income for the current period (Income Summary Account).
--   Calculate the debit-credit difference and add it as NetIncome or
--   NetLoss to the plank Note.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s = total number of scalar entries)
incomeSummaryAccount :: (Note n, HatVal v, ExBaseClass b) => Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | Net income transfer (Journal version). Transfer NetIncome/NetLoss to
--   RetainedEarnings for each Note.
--   
--   Complexity: O(j * s) (j = number of Notes, s = number of scalar
--   entries per Note)
netIncomeTransfer :: (Note n, HatVal v, ExBaseClass b) => Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | Gross profit transfer (Journal version). Aggregate sales and cost
--   accounts into GrossProfit for each Note.
--   
--   Complexity: O(j * s)
grossProfitTransfer :: (Note n, HatVal v, ExBaseClass b) => Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | Ordinary Profit Transfer
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; type Test = Journal String Double (HatBase (CountUnit, AccountTitles))
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; x = 2279.0:@Not:&lt;(Yen,Depreciation) .| "A" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; y = 500475.0:@Not:&lt;(Yen,InterestEarned) .| "B" :: Test
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; ExchangeAlgebra.Journal.Transfer.ordinaryProfitTransfer ( x .+ y)
--   2279.00:@Hat:&lt;(Yen,OrdinaryProfit).|"A" .+ 500475.00:@Not:&lt;(Yen,OrdinaryProfit).|"B"
--   </pre>
ordinaryProfitTransfer :: (Note n, HatVal v, ExBaseClass b) => Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | Retained earnings transfer (Journal version). Transfer OrdinaryProfit
--   to RetainedEarnings for each Note.
--   
--   Complexity: O(j * s)
retainedEarningTransfer :: (Note n, HatVal v, ExBaseClass b) => Journal n v b -> Journal n v b

-- | Income summary account (Journal version). Transfer all cost and
--   revenue accounts to RetainedEarnings, then offset using the Bar
--   operation.
--   
--   Complexity: O(j * s)
finalStockTransfer :: (Note n, HatVal v, ExBaseClass b) => Journal n v b -> Journal n v b


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping system.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
module ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate

-- | Type class defining the time axis for simulations.
--   
--   <tt>nextTerm</tt> and <tt>prevTerm</tt> have default implementations
--   via <tt>succ</tt> / <tt>pred</tt>, so for uniformly-spaced time axes
--   only <tt>initTerm</tt> and <tt>lastTerm</tt> need to be defined.
--   
--   <pre>
--   type Term = Int
--   
--   instance StateTime Term where
--       initTerm = 1
--       lastTerm = 100
--       -- nextTerm and prevTerm use the defaults (succ, pred)
--   </pre>
class (Eq t, Show t, Ord t, Enum t, Ix t) => StateTime t
initTerm :: StateTime t => t
lastTerm :: StateTime t => t

-- | Return the next term. Defaults to <tt>succ</tt>.
nextTerm :: StateTime t => t -> t

-- | Return the previous term. Defaults to <tt>pred</tt>.
prevTerm :: StateTime t => t -> t

-- | Update pattern for advancing to the next term. Specifies how each
--   Updatable field is updated.
data UpdatePattern

-- | Copy the previous term's state as-is
Copy :: UpdatePattern

-- | Update by modifying the previous term's state
Modify :: UpdatePattern

-- | Do nothing (used when all terms are pre-generated during
--   initialization)
DoNothing :: UpdatePattern

-- | Type class for updatable fields that constitute a StateSpace. Provides
--   an interface for initialization, copying, and modification. Through
--   Generic-based automatic derivation, initAll/updateAll of StateSpace
--   are auto-implemented.
class (StateTime t, InitVariables v) => Updatable t v (a :: Type -> Type) s | a s -> t v where {
    type Inner a s;
}
unwrap :: Updatable t v a s => a s -> Inner a s
initialize :: Updatable t v a s => StdGen -> t -> v -> ST s (a s)
updatePattern :: Updatable t v a s => a s -> ST s UpdatePattern
copy :: Updatable t v a s => StdGen -> t -> v -> a s -> ST s ()
modify :: Updatable t v a s => StdGen -> t -> v -> a s -> ST s ()
update :: Updatable t v a s => StdGen -> t -> v -> a s -> ST s ()
initAll :: StateSpace t v e a s => StdGen -> t -> v -> ST s (a s)
updateAll :: StateSpace t v e a s => StdGen -> t -> v -> a s -> ST s ()

-- | Type class defining the world state for a simulation. Initialize with
--   initAll, update state each term with updateAll, and execute event
--   processing with event. Through DefaultSignatures with Generic, initAll
--   and updateAll can be automatically derived.
class (StateTime t, InitVariables v, Event e) => StateSpace t v e (a :: Type -> Type) s | a s -> t v e
event :: StateSpace t v e a s => a s -> t -> e -> ST s ()

-- | Define the random seed (default 42). The random seed can also be
--   explicitly defined. Currently unused.
randomSeeds :: StateSpace t v e a s => a s -> ST s Int

-- | Generate a random number following the standard normal distribution
--   N(0,1). Uses the Box-Muller method.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
normal :: (RandomGen g, Random a, Floating a) => g -> (a, g)

-- | Generate a random number following the normal distribution N(mean,
--   sigma) with specified mean and standard deviation.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
normal' :: (RandomGen g, Random a, Floating a) => (a, a) -> g -> (a, g)

-- | Advance the random number generator a specified number of times. When
--   sharing a single random number generator across multiple variables,
--   repeat the advancement an appropriate number of times to avoid
--   generating the same random numbers, especially during initialization.
--   
--   Complexity: O(n)
updateGen :: RandomGen g => g -> Int -> g

-- | Type class for initialization parameters. Parameters referenced during
--   initialization at the start of a simulation. Values referenced during
--   simulation execution should be included in <a>Updatable</a>.
class (Eq e, Show e) => InitVariables e

-- | Type class for newtype wrappers around <tt>STRef</tt>.
--   
--   <tt>_unwrapURef</tt> and <tt>_wrapURef</tt> have
--   <tt>Coercible</tt>-based default implementations, so newtype wrappers
--   only need an empty instance declaration.
--   
--   <pre>
--   newtype SP s = SP (STRef s Double)
--   
--   -- Empty instance is sufficient thanks to Coercible defaults
--   instance UpdatableSTRef SP s Double
--   
--   -- Previous boilerplate (no longer needed):
--   -- instance UpdatableSTRef SP s Double where
--   --     _unwrapURef (SP x) = x
--   --     _wrapURef x = SP x
--   </pre>
class UpdatableSTRef wrapper s b | wrapper s -> b

-- | Unwrap to the inner <tt>STRef</tt>. For newtype wrappers, the
--   <tt>Coercible</tt> default is used.
_unwrapURef :: UpdatableSTRef wrapper s b => wrapper s -> STRef s b

-- | Unwrap to the inner <tt>STRef</tt>. For newtype wrappers, the
--   <tt>Coercible</tt> default is used.
_unwrapURef :: (UpdatableSTRef wrapper s b, Coercible (wrapper s) (STRef s b)) => wrapper s -> STRef s b

-- | Wrap an <tt>STRef</tt> into the newtype. For newtype wrappers, the
--   <tt>Coercible</tt> default is used.
_wrapURef :: UpdatableSTRef wrapper s b => STRef s b -> wrapper s

-- | Wrap an <tt>STRef</tt> into the newtype. For newtype wrappers, the
--   <tt>Coercible</tt> default is used.
_wrapURef :: (UpdatableSTRef wrapper s b, Coercible (STRef s b) (wrapper s)) => STRef s b -> wrapper s
newURef :: UpdatableSTRef wrapper s b => b -> ST s (wrapper s)
readURef :: UpdatableSTRef wrapper s b => wrapper s -> ST s b
writeURef :: UpdatableSTRef wrapper s b => wrapper s -> b -> ST s ()
modifyURef :: UpdatableSTRef wrapper s b => wrapper s -> (b -> b) -> ST s ()

-- | Type class for newtype wrappers around <tt>STArray</tt>.
--   
--   <tt>_unwrapUArray</tt> and <tt>_wrapUArray</tt> have
--   <tt>Coercible</tt>-based default implementations, so newtype wrappers
--   only need an empty instance declaration.
--   
--   <pre>
--   newtype ICTable s = ICTable (STArray s (Int, Int) Double)
--   
--   -- Empty instance is sufficient thanks to Coercible defaults
--   instance UpdatableSTArray ICTable s (Int, Int) Double
--   
--   -- Previous boilerplate (no longer needed):
--   -- instance UpdatableSTArray ICTable s (Int, Int) Double where
--   --     _unwrapUArray (ICTable arr) = arr
--   --     _wrapUArray arr = ICTable arr
--   </pre>
class (Ix b) => UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c | wrapper s -> b c

-- | Unwrap to the inner <tt>STArray</tt>. For newtype wrappers, the
--   <tt>Coercible</tt> default is used.
_unwrapUArray :: UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c => wrapper s -> STArray s b c

-- | Unwrap to the inner <tt>STArray</tt>. For newtype wrappers, the
--   <tt>Coercible</tt> default is used.
_unwrapUArray :: (UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c, Coercible (wrapper s) (STArray s b c)) => wrapper s -> STArray s b c

-- | Wrap an <tt>STArray</tt> into the newtype. For newtype wrappers, the
--   <tt>Coercible</tt> default is used.
_wrapUArray :: UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c => STArray s b c -> wrapper s

-- | Wrap an <tt>STArray</tt> into the newtype. For newtype wrappers, the
--   <tt>Coercible</tt> default is used.
_wrapUArray :: (UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c, Coercible (STArray s b c) (wrapper s)) => STArray s b c -> wrapper s
getUBounds :: UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c => wrapper s -> ST s (b, b)
newUArray :: UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c => (b, b) -> c -> ST s (wrapper s)
readUArray :: UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c => wrapper s -> b -> ST s c
writeUArray :: UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c => wrapper s -> b -> c -> ST s ()
modifyUArray :: UpdatableSTArray wrapper s b c => wrapper s -> b -> (c -> c) -> ST s ()

-- | Modify the value at a given index of an array using a function.
--   Evaluates strictly before writing back.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
modifyArray :: (MArray a t m, Ix i) => a i t -> i -> (t -> t) -> m ()

-- | Type class for event types. Used to enumerate events executed at each
--   term of the simulation. By default, <a>minBound</a> / <a>maxBound</a>
--   from <a>Bounded</a> are used.
class (Ord e, Show e, Enum e, Eq e, Bounded e) => Event e

-- | The first event. Defaults to <tt>minBound</tt>.
fstEvent :: Event e => e

-- | The last event. Defaults to <tt>maxBound</tt>.
lastEvent :: Event e => e

-- | Execute all events in order. Calls <tt>event</tt> from
--   <tt>fstEvent</tt> through <tt>lastEvent</tt>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|events| * cost(event))
eventAll :: forall t v e a s. StateSpace t v e a s => a s -> t -> ST s ()

-- | Spill configuration for periodic external logging. <a>spillExtract</a>
--   selects accounting payload from world. <a>spillWriteChunk</a> controls
--   on-disk format.
data SpillOptions t a payload
SpillOptions :: !Int -> FilePath -> (a RealWorld -> ST RealWorld payload) -> Maybe ((t, t) -> a RealWorld -> ST RealWorld payload) -> (Handle -> (t, t) -> payload -> IO ()) -> SpillDeletePolicy t -> ((t, t) -> a RealWorld -> ST RealWorld ()) -> SpillOptions t a payload
[spillEveryTerms] :: SpillOptions t a payload -> !Int
[spillFilePath] :: SpillOptions t a payload -> FilePath
[spillExtract] :: SpillOptions t a payload -> a RealWorld -> ST RealWorld payload
[spillExtractChunk] :: SpillOptions t a payload -> Maybe ((t, t) -> a RealWorld -> ST RealWorld payload)
[spillWriteChunk] :: SpillOptions t a payload -> Handle -> (t, t) -> payload -> IO ()
