[spillDeletePolicy] :: SpillOptions t a payload -> SpillDeletePolicy t
[spillDeleteRange] :: SpillOptions t a payload -> (t, t) -> a RealWorld -> ST RealWorld ()

-- | Policy to decide which term range to evict after each spill.
data SpillDeletePolicy t
NoDelete :: SpillDeletePolicy t
DeleteSpilledChunk :: SpillDeletePolicy t
KeepRecentTerms :: Int -> SpillDeletePolicy t

-- | Construct text-format SpillOptions. interval is the spill interval (in
--   terms), path is the output file path.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
mkSpillOptions :: Show t => Int -> FilePath -> (a RealWorld -> ST RealWorld String) -> SpillOptions t a String

-- | Construct binary-format SpillOptions. Spills in a format that can be
--   restored with <a>readBinarySpillFile</a>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(1)
mkBinarySpillOptions :: (Binary t, Binary payload) => Int -> FilePath -> (a RealWorld -> ST RealWorld payload) -> SpillOptions t a payload

-- | Default text-format spill writer. Writes the chunk range and payload
--   as text to the handle.
defaultSpillWriter :: Show t => Handle -> (t, t) -> String -> IO ()

-- | Default binary-format spill writer. Writes the chunk range and payload
--   to the handle using <a>encode</a>.
defaultBinarySpillWriter :: (Binary t, Binary payload) => Handle -> (t, t) -> payload -> IO ()

-- | Read a binary spill file and return it as a list of chunks. Used to
--   restore files written by <a>defaultBinarySpillWriter</a>. When
--   decoding fails, the remaining data is truncated.
--   
--   Complexity: O(file size)
readBinarySpillFile :: (Binary t, Binary payload) => FilePath -> IO [((t, t), payload)]

-- | Run a simulation from initialization through the final term. Build the
--   world state with initAll, then repeat updateAll followed by eventAll
--   for each term.
--   
--   Complexity: O(T * (updateAll + eventAll)) (T = number of terms)
runSimulation :: StateSpace t v e a RealWorld => StdGen -> v -> IO (a RealWorld)

-- | Run simulation with periodic spill. This function keeps existing
--   behavior and additionally writes extracted accounting payload every N
--   terms (and at the last term).
runSimulationWithSpill :: StateSpace t v e a RealWorld => SpillOptions t a payload -> StdGen -> v -> IO (a RealWorld)

-- | Run multiple simulation scenarios concurrently and return results as a
--   <tt>Map</tt>.
--   
--   Each scenario is a <tt>(name, parameters)</tt> pair. All scenarios
--   share the same random seed and are executed in parallel.
--   
--   <pre>
--   let gen = mkStdGen 2025
--       scenarios = [("default", defaultEnv), ("plus", plusEnv)]
--   results &lt;- runScenarios gen scenarios
--   -- results :: Map String (World RealWorld)
--   </pre>
runScenarios :: StateSpace t v e a RealWorld => StdGen -> [(String, v)] -> IO (Map String (a RealWorld))

-- | Run multiple simulation scenarios concurrently with periodic spill.
--   
--   The spill options builder receives the scenario name, allowing
--   per-scenario file paths and logging.
--   
--   <pre>
--   results &lt;- runScenariosWithSpill
--       (\name -&gt; mkBinarySpillOptions 50 (dir ++ name ++ ".bin") extract)
--       gen
--       scenarios
--   </pre>
runScenariosWithSpill :: StateSpace t v e a RealWorld => (String -> SpillOptions t a payload) -> StdGen -> [(String, v)] -> IO (Map String (a RealWorld))

-- | Calculate Leontief's Inverse Matrix ex. main :: IO () main = do mat
--   &lt;- newListArray ((1,1),(2,2)) [0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.1] result &lt;-
--   leontiefInverse mat putStrLn "Leontief Inverse (ripple effect
--   matrix):" writeLeontiefInverse "output.csv" result
leontiefInverse :: IOArray (Int, Int) Double -> IO (IOArray (Int, Int) Double)

-- | Calculate the ripple effect of a demand increase in a specific
--   industry using the inverse matrix. Returns the ripple effect on each
--   industry when a one-unit demand increase occurs in the specified
--   industry.
--   
--   Complexity: O(n) (n = number of industries)
rippleEffect :: Int -> IOArray (Int, Int) Double -> IO (IOArray (Int, Int) Double)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.UpdatePattern
instance GHC.Show.Show ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.UpdatePattern
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.StateTime t, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.InitVariables v) => ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v GHC.Generics.U1 s
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.StateTime t, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.InitVariables v, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.Updatable t v a s) => ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v (GHC.Generics.K1 i (a s)) s
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.StateTime t, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.InitVariables v, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v p s, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v q s) => ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v (p GHC.Generics.:+: q) s
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.StateTime t, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.InitVariables v, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v p s, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v q s) => ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v (p GHC.Generics.:*: q) s
instance (ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.StateTime t, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.InitVariables v, ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v f s) => ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.GUpdatable t v (GHC.Generics.M1 p l f) s


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping system.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
--   
--   _Note_ : The current version 0.1.0.0 will be completely changed
--   shortly, especially in the accounts settings section.
module ExchangeAlgebra.Simulate.Visualize

-- | Split STArray data into a grid with 3 columns and return as a
--   GridMatrix. Each cell contains the time series data for one agent
--   (index).
--   
--   Complexity: O(|agents| * T) (T = number of terms)
gridLine :: (Ord a, Show a, Ix a, StateTime t) => STArray s (a, t) Double -> ST s (GridMatrix t)

-- | Plot the specified output of each agent as a line graph divided into a
--   grid. Splits into grid columns of 3, and outputs as a PNG file.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|range| * T) (T = number of terms)
plotLine :: (StateTime t, Enum t, PlotValue t, Ord i, Show i, Ix i, Enum i) => (a RealWorld -> t -> i -> ST RealWorld Double) -> (i, i) -> a RealWorld -> FileName -> Title -> IO ()

-- | Plot the results of multiple worlds/functions with color coding in
--   each grid cell. Used for comparing different scenarios.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|worlds| * |range| * T)
plotMultiLines :: (StateTime t, Enum t, PlotValue t, Ord i, Show i, Ix i, Enum i) => [Label] -> [a RealWorld -> t -> i -> ST RealWorld Double] -> (i, i) -> [a RealWorld] -> FilePath -> String -> IO ()

-- | Plot the difference between two worlds in a grid. Each cell plots
--   <tt>f wld1 t i - f wld2 t i</tt>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|range| * T)
plotWldsDiffLine :: (StateTime t, Enum t, PlotValue t, Ord i, Show i, Ix i, Enum i) => (a RealWorld -> t -> i -> ST RealWorld Double) -> (i, i) -> (a RealWorld, a RealWorld) -> FilePath -> String -> IO ()

-- | Draw line graphs using Unboxed Vectors. Equivalent to <a>plotLine</a>,
--   but uses Unboxed Vectors instead of STArray internally, providing
--   better memory efficiency for large-scale data.
--   
--   Complexity: O(|range| * T)
plotLineVector :: (Enum i, Enum t, StateTime t, Show i, PlotValue t) => (a RealWorld -> t -> i -> ST RealWorld Double) -> ((i, t), (i, t)) -> a RealWorld -> FilePath -> String -> IO ()

-- | Output the results of given functions as CSV time series data.
--   Internally uses streaming output via
--   <a>writeFuncResultsWithContext</a>.
--   
--   Complexity: O(T * |funcs| * cost(f))
writeFuncResults :: (StateTime t, Show x, Num x) => [(Header, a RealWorld -> t -> ST RealWorld x)] -> (t, t) -> a RealWorld -> FilePath -> IO ()

-- | Build a context for each term, then evaluate multiple functions
--   together and output to CSV. By sharing each term's context, expensive
--   preprocessing (e.g., termJournal, transfer) is reduced to once per
--   term. Uses streaming output, so memory usage does not depend on the
--   number of terms.
--   
--   Complexity: O(T * (cost(buildCtx) + |funcs| * cost(f)))
writeFuncResultsWithContext :: (StateTime t, Show x, Num x) => (a RealWorld -> t -> ST RealWorld c) -> [(Header, c -> ST RealWorld x)] -> (t, t) -> a RealWorld -> FilePath -> IO ()


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping system.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
--   
--   _Note_ : The current version 0.1.0.0 will be completely changed
--   shortly, especially in the accounts settings section.
module ExchangeAlgebra.Write

-- | Helper to convert from Show to Text.
--   
--   Complexity: O(show cost)
tshow :: Show a => a -> Text

-- | Output a Balance Sheet in CSV format. Internally applies
--   <tt>finalStockTransfer</tt>, then decomposes into assets, liabilities,
--   and equity for output.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s = total number of scalar entries)
writeBS :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b, ExBaseClass b) => FilePath -> Alg n b -> IO ()

-- | Output a Profit and Loss Statement in CSV format. Decomposes into
--   costs and revenues for output.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s) (s = total number of scalar entries)
writePL :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b, ExBaseClass b) => FilePath -> Alg n b -> IO ()

-- | Pad two lists to the same length. Appends empty text to the shorter
--   list.
--   
--   Complexity: O(max(|xs|, |ys|))
toSameLength :: [Text] -> [Text] -> ([Text], [Text])

-- | Output journal entries in CSV format. Groups by date and records the
--   debit/credit account titles and amounts for each day.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s * log d) (s = number of entries, d = number of
--   distinct dates)
writeJournal :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b, ExBaseClass b) => FilePath -> Alg n b -> (b -> Day) -> IO ()

-- | Output account ledgers in CSV format.
--   
--   <b>Note</b>: Not yet implemented. Calling this will raise an
--   exception.
writeAccountOf :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b, ExBaseClass b) => [AccountTitles] -> FilePath -> Alg n b -> (b -> Day) -> IO ()

-- | Output a Compound Trial Balance in CSV format. Calculates the debit
--   total, credit total, and balance for each account title and outputs as
--   a table.
--   
--   Complexity: O(s * a) (s = number of entries, a = number of distinct
--   account titles)
writeCompoundTrialBalance :: (HatVal n, HatBaseClass b, ExBaseClass b) => FilePath -> Alg n b -> IO ()

-- | Output the Input-Output Table for a specified term in CSV format.
--   Outputs a slice of the specified term from a 3D array (term, row
--   industry, column industry).
--   
--   Complexity: O(r * c) (r = number of rows, c = number of columns)
writeTermIO :: (HatVal n, BaseClass b, StateTime t, Ix b, Ix t, Enum b) => FilePath -> t -> IOArray (t, b, b) n -> IO ()

-- | Output a 2D IOArray (Input-Output Table or ripple effect matrix) in
--   CSV format.
--   
--   Complexity: O(r * c) (r = number of rows, c = number of columns)
writeIOMatrix :: FilePath -> IOArray (Int, Int) Double -> IO ()

-- | Restore a complete Journal from spilled binary chunks and the current
--   in-memory Journal. The in-memory portion is narrowed to only terms
--   after the last spill range, so duplicate terms are not double-counted.
--   
--   Complexity: O(file size + number of chunks * union cost)
restoreJournalFromBinarySpill :: (Binary t, Ord t, Binary (Journal n v b), Note n, HatVal v, HatBaseClass b) => FilePath -> (n -> t) -> Journal n v b -> IO (Journal n v b)


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping systems.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
--   
--   _Note_ : The current version 0.1.0.0 will be completely changed
--   shortly, especially in the accounts settings section.
module ExchangeAlgebra


-- | Released under the OWL license
--   
--   Package for Exchange Algebra defined by Hiroshi Deguchi.
--   
--   Exchange Algebra is an algebraic description of bookkeeping systems.
--   Details are below.
--   
--   <a>https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9784431209850</a>
--   
--   
--   <a>https://repository.kulib.kyoto-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/2433/82987/1/0809-7.pdf</a>
--   
--   _Note_ : The current version 0.1.0.0 will be completely changed
--   shortly, especially in the accounts settings section.
module ExchangeAlgebraJournal
